title: Commission opens antitrust investigation into Align Technology's conduct regarding Invisalign clear aligners and intra-oral scanners
content: The European Commission has opened a formal antitrust investigation to assess whether Align Technology has breached EU competition rules by tying its intra-oral scanners to its dental clear aligners branded as Invisalign in the European Economic Area ('EEA').
snippet: The European Commission has opened a formal antitrust investigation to assess whether Align Technology has breached EU competition rules by tying its intra-oral scanners to its dental clear aligners

title: Changes to integration measures and research on migrant youth and housing in Slovakia
content: Temporary protection status for Ukrainian refugees in Slovakia was automatically extended until March 4, 2027. Additionally, the Ministry of Interior prepared a new law on international protection to replace the current asylum framework, aiming to streamline procedures and align with EU standards.

title: Sustainable cities: Eight finalists announced for 2028 European Green Capital and Green Leaf Awards
content: The Commission has shortlisted eight out of 27 eligible cities for the final stage of the European Green Capital and Green Leaf Awards.
snippet: The Commission has shortlisted eight out of 27 eligible cities for the final stage of the European Green Capital and Green Leaf Awards.

title: Research and policies on migrant integration in Denmark
content: In Denmark’s health sector, foreign nationals (including TCNs) play a growing role in sustaining staffing levels, according to a new analysis by the Danish Chamber of Commerce. Denmark allows workers from 16 non-EU countries to access employment under more flexible conditions than other TCNs.

title: Sentinel-1A concludes its mission, leaving a lasting legacy for Copernicus
content: After more than 12 years in orbit, Copernicus Sentinel 1A has concluded its operational mission, marking the end of a pioneering chapter in the European Union’s Earth observation programme.
snippet: After more than 12 years in orbit, Copernicus Sentinel 1A has concluded its operational mission, marking the end of a pioneering chapter in the European Union’s Earth observation programme.

title: Commission welcomes political agreement on criminal law rules to fight child sexual abuse
content: On 22 June, the European Parliament and Council reached a political agreement on the Directive updating the criminal law rules on child sexual abuse and sexual exploitation, a major milestone in the protection of children as worrying new developments make the agreement all the more timely.

title: Eurostat: Renewable energy and natural gas supply up in 2025
content: In 2025, according to preliminary data, the supply of natural gas and renewable energy in the EU grew compared with 2024, while the supply of coal and petroleum products decreased.
snippet: In 2025, according to preliminary data, the supply of natural gas and renewable energy in the EU grew compared with 2024, while the supply of coal and petroleum products decreased.

title: Adoption of the reform of the Research Fund for Coal and Steel
content: Today, the Council of the EU adopted two Decisions reforming the Research Fund for Coal and Steel.
snippet: Today, the Council of the EU adopted two Decisions reforming the Research Fund for Coal and Steel.

title: CEF Energy: 6th call for Cross-Border Renewable Energy Projects to obtain the status launched
content: On 29 June, CINEA launched the 6th call for projects aiming to obtain the Cross-Border Renewable Energy (CB RES) status, the last status call under the current 2021–2027 Multiannual Financial Framework (MFF).

title: High Representative Kallas with Commissioners Kos and Brunner in Türkiye to advance bilateral cooperation
content: Today and tomorrow, High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy and Vice-President of the European Commission, Kaja Kallas, Commissioner for Enlargement, Marta Kos, and Commissioner for Internal Affairs and Migration, Magnus Brunner, will travel to Türkiye...

title: New rules to protect EU steel industry from damaging impacts of global overcapacity enter into application
content: A new regulation that protects the EU steel sector from the damaging impacts of global overcapacity enters into application on 1 July 2026. This represents a vital step towards ensuring the long-term viability of a strategically crucial European industry.

title: EU and Canada conclude the Energy Security Business Roundtable to deepen transatlantic business relations
content: Montreal, 29 June - Today, the European Commission and Canada convened the EU-Canada Energy Security Business Roundtable on the sidelines of the 11th IEA Energy Efficiency Conference in Montreal, reaffirming their commitment to a stronger transatlantic energy partnership.

title: Policy and research developments in migrant integration in the Czech Republic
content: The Czech government approved another registration round for special long-term residence permits for temporary protection holders as the Ministry of Finance, the Ministry of Labour and Social Affairs and the Ministry of Interior are launching an initiative to reduce illegal and disguised employment.

title: Impressive Defence Enablers present at first ENDR Newcomers’ Day
content: On 25/6/2026, the first online event “ENDR Newcomers' Day” took place, where 13 new members joining ENDR during the last months presented themselves.
snippet: On 25/6/2026, the first online event “ENDR Newcomers' Day” took place, where 13 new members joining ENDR during the last months presented themselves.

title: EU funded project in Estonia aims to make the internet safer for children
content: The EU funded project “Preventing children from becoming victims of sexual abuse online” in Estonia helps children to develop skills to navigate the digital world safely and confidently, using tools like an illustrated book, a board game, and training for educators and law enforcement.

title: Irish Council Presidency includes a focus on security and migration
content: Ireland will hold the Presidency of the Council of the EU from 1 July to 31 December 2026. Under the slogan “Ní neart go cur le chéile - Strength with unity”, the Irish Presidency will focus on three core priorities: competitiveness, values and security.

title: First European research network on antisemitism and Jewish life officially launched
content: Research and innovation news alert: A new Horizon Europe project will build a European research hub to address contemporary antisemitism and foster Jewish life.
snippet: Research and innovation news alert: A new Horizon Europe project will build a European research hub to address contemporary antisemitism and foster Jewish life.

title: Commission presents financial package for Montenegro's EU accession
content: Today, the European Commission adopted a financial package setting out the budgetary arrangements that would apply to Montenegro upon its accession to the European Union. Montenegro advances in the accession process and this proposal marks a significant step in the country's path towards becoming...

title: Commission clarifies rules on plastic bottles recycling
content: The measures will ensure transparency in calculating recycled content in new single-use polyethylene terephthalate (PET) bottles.
snippet: The measures will ensure transparency in calculating recycled content in new single-use polyethylene terephthalate (PET) bottles.

title: Commissioner Kadis hosts implementation dialogue on EU aquaculture policy
content: Today, Costas Kadis, Commissioner for Fisheries and Oceans, is hosting an implementation dialogue on EU aquaculture policy. The event brings together stakeholders from across the sector – including aquaculture industry and associations, retailers, non-governmental organisations, civil society

title: Commission disburses €3.9 billion for drones under the €90 billion Ukraine Support Loan
content: The European Commission begins disbursement today of €3.9 billion as the first payment under the first tranche of around €6 billion dedicated to drone procurement, a key capability enabling Ukraine to withstand Russia’s war of aggression.

title: Commission approves €402 million Spanish State aid for road transport companies facing increased fuel prices
content:
The European Commission has approved a €402 million Spanish State aid scheme to support road
transport companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.
snippet: The European Commission has approved a €402 million Spanish State aid scheme to support road transport companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.

title: Commission presents Council with ambitious Indonesia agreements
content: Today the European Commission presented its proposals on the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) and the Investment Protection Agreement (IPA) with Indonesia to the Council for their signature and conclusion.

title: Lithuania receives first €956.3 million payment under SAFE
content: On 24 June 2026, Lithuania received its first payment of € 956.3 million under the Security Action for Europe (SAFE) defence financing instrument, representing 15% of its total allocation of €6.4 billion.
snippet: On 24 June 2026, Lithuania received its first payment of € 956.3 million under the Security Action for Europe (SAFE) defence financing instrument, representing 15% of its total allocation of €6.4

title: What the latest data reveals about labour market imbalances across Europe
content: Europe faces a massive labour mismatch. To bridge the gap between shortages and surpluses, we need smarter reforms and mobility.
snippet: Europe faces a massive labour mismatch. To bridge the gap between shortages and surpluses, we need smarter reforms and mobility.

title: Your Voice on EU Enlargement: call for organisations to host outreach and engagement events
content: The European Commission has launched “Your Voice on EU Enlargement”, a call for applications to organize outreach and engagement events on EU enlargement in EU Member States.
snippet: The European Commission has launched “Your Voice on EU Enlargement”, a call for applications to organize outreach and engagement events on EU enlargement in EU Member States.

title: Casting his own line: A young fisher’s path to independence in Réunion
content: At just 27, Richard Delmas is charting his own course in the waters off Réunion Island. Born into a fishing family, he grew up at sea. Yet he chose not to pursue his father’s large-scale fishing operations. Instead, with support from the EU, he launched a small one-person fishing business

title: Commission calls on the Netherlands to improve competition for international rail passenger services
content: The European Commission has informed the Netherlands that the rules for the allocation of capacity on the Dutch main railway network may be in breach of EU antitrust rules.
snippet: The European Commission has informed the Netherlands that the rules for the allocation of capacity on the Dutch main railway network may be in breach of EU antitrust rules.

title: Ensuring fairness and safety: €3 customs duty for low-value parcels
content: From 1 July 2026, the EU will introduce a temporary €3 customs duty on low-value parcels imported from outside the EU, mainly through e-commerce.
snippet: From 1 July 2026, the EU will introduce a temporary €3 customs duty on low-value parcels imported from outside the EU, mainly through e-commerce.
